title: VimFu — g? ROT13 Encoding
description: g? applies ROT13 encoding to text. Each letter shifts 13 positions in the alphabet. Apply it twice to get back the original.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Spoiler Alert
The butler did it.
The cake is a lie.
Soylent green is people.

steps.say: G question mark applies ROT thirteen encoding. Each letter shifts thirteen places in the alphabet. A becomes N, B becomes O, and so on.
Line two. The butler did it. G question mark, question mark. Encode the line.
Scrambled. Unreadable. The spoiler is hidden.
Apply it again to decode. G question mark, question mark.
Back to the original. ROT thirteen is its own inverse. Apply twice to get back.
Encode the whole file. G G, then G question mark, G. From top to bottom.
G question mark. ROT thirteen operator. Fun for hiding spoilers. Not real encryption though.
